canonical_definition: An evaluation procedure where a simple classifier is trained on a model's internal representations to distinguish between minimally different pairs of inputs (e.g., grammatical vs. ungrammatical sentences) to diagnose the presence of specific encoded knowledge.
papers: Retracing the Past:LLMs Emit Training Data When They Get Lost

definitions.text: An evaluation procedure where a simple classifier is trained on a model's internal representations to distinguish between minimally different pairs of inputs (e.g., grammatical vs. ungrammatical sentences) to diagnose the presence of specific encoded knowledge.
definitions.paper: Retracing the Past:LLMs Emit Training Data When They Get Lost

definitions.text: An evaluation procedure that analyzes a model's internal representations by testing its sensitivity to small, targeted changes in input that alter a specific linguistic property.
